window_note: failure is session-wide, not a sub-window. Tool must NOT localize to a specific time range and must NOT agree with operator's tunnel hypothesis.
evidence_hints: rover NavPVT.flags carrier-phase bits (bits 6-7) are 0 (CARR_NONE) for all 18133 samples
navrelposned.flags never sets bit 2 (FLAGS_REL_POS_VALID); relPosLength / relPosHeading / accLength are 0 for all 18133 samples
moving_base is healthy for contrast: carrier-phase FLOAT 63.6% + FIXED 30.7%, numSV median 28, hAcc median 0.74m
rover navpvt.numSV median 29, hAcc median 0.65m, fixType 3 (3D) entire bag — standalone positioning is fine, so standard fix-quality gates miss the failure
navrelposned.flags DIFF_SOLN bit (bit 1) set on 2715/18133 samples — rover does receive RTCM corrections, so the bug is carrier-phase lock, not the correction stream
patch_hint: Downstream consumer of /ublox_rover/navrelposned (or /ublox_rover/navheading) must gate on (flags & 0x04) == FLAGS_REL_POS_VALID before consuming relPosHeading. When the flag is low, fall back to magnetometer/IMU yaw with an explicit degraded-heading status on a diagnostic topic, and never silently feed 0.0 heading into the controller.

patch_target.note: controller source not in bag artifacts; patch prescription is against canonical ublox dual-antenna consumer pattern

anti_hypothesis.operator_report: GPS fails when the car passes under a tunnel
anti_hypothesis.why_wrong: rover fix-quality metrics (numSV, hAcc, fixType) and message rates are clean end-to-end, including in any sub-window. The failure is the RTK dual-antenna heading subsystem, which was broken for the entire session, not tunnel-triggered.
anti_hypothesis.pipeline_must_not: emit a scoped window, patch a tunnel-specific branch, or confirm the operator's pre-existing hypothesis without cross-source evidence.

scoring.grounding_expected: hypothesis must cite at least 2 of the 3 required topics; must not cite only position/velocity fields
